skill.name: web-design-guidelines
skill.description: Review UI files against Vercel Web Interface Guidelines for accessibility, interaction quality, layout, and visual design issues.
skill.summary: Audit web interfaces against current Vercel design and accessibility guidance.
skill.author: vercel-labs

security_audit.summary: The static external command and weak cryptography findings are false positives caused by Markdown backticks, front matter, and prose. The confirmed risk is the skill's instruction to fetch mutable remote guidelines and use the fetched content as review rules and output instructions. No evidence found of malicious prompt injection, credential exfiltration, filesystem writes, or executable code.

medium_findings.title: Mutable Remote Guidelines Control Review Behavior
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to fetch a raw GitHub URL before each review and use the fetched content for rules and output format instructions. This is a legitimate design-review workflow, but the remote content is mutable and can change the agent's behavior after publication.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network dependency is explicit and semantically relevant because fetched content supplies review rules and output instructions. I found no evidence that the current URL is malicious, so the risk is mutable remote control rather than confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Markdown
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown formatting, not executable code. The reported weak cryptography locations are front matter or ordinary prose, and no cryptographic operation is present at those lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain YAML metadata, prose, inline code formatting, and a fenced URL block. There is no executable Ruby, shell command, hash function, or encryption API in the skill file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Runtime WebFetch of Instruction Content
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill requires fetching fresh remote content during each review and treating that content as authoritative instructions. This pattern can expose users to changed or compromised upstream instructions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file directly instructs use of WebFetch and states that fetched content contains rules and output format instructions. The risk depends on future remote content changes, so confidence is high for the pattern and moderate for exploitability.

content.user_title: Audit Web Interfaces Against Design Guidelines
content.value_statement: UI reviews often miss consistent accessibility, layout, and interaction details. This skill checks selected files against Vercel guidelines and reports concise findings.
actual_capabilities: Fetches the latest Vercel Web Interface Guidelines before a review.
Reads user-specified files or file patterns for interface review.
Checks UI implementation against accessibility and design rules.
Reports findings in a concise file and line format.
Asks the user for target files when no file or pattern is provided.
limitations: Requires network access to retrieve the latest guideline document.
Depends on the availability and integrity of the remote GitHub source.
Reviews source files and cannot fully replace manual browser testing.
Does not include its own fixed guideline copy for offline audits.

use_cases.title: Pre-merge UI Review
use_cases.description: Check changed frontend files before a pull request is merged.

use_cases.title: Accessibility Pass
use_cases.description: Find interface issues that may affect keyboard use, contrast, or readable structure.

use_cases.title: Design Consistency Audit
use_cases.description: Review pages and components for consistency with shared web interface standards.

prompt_templates.title: Review One File
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my UI in app/page.tsx against the Web Interface Guidelines.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single page or component.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Feature Folder
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the files in app/settings for accessibility, layout, and interaction issues.
prompt_templates.scenario: Review a small product area before release.

prompt_templates.title: Review Changed UI Files
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the changed frontend files in this branch and report only guideline violations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused pull request review for UI quality.

prompt_templates.title: Prioritize Release Blockers
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the checkout UI and prioritize findings that could block release or harm usability.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review that emphasizes severity and release risk.

output_examples.input: Review app/dashboard/page.tsx for Web Interface Guidelines compliance.
output_examples.output: Reports concise findings with file and line references, such as contrast, focus state, spacing, or responsive layout issues.

output_examples.input: Audit components/navigation for accessibility and interaction quality.
output_examples.output: Lists guideline violations by location and keeps the response focused on actionable UI fixes.

output_examples.input: Check the checkout flow before release.
output_examples.output: Highlights severe usability and accessibility concerns first, then includes lower priority interface polish items.

best_practices: Provide specific files or narrow patterns so findings include useful locations.
Run the review after major UI changes and before final pull request approval.
Pair the results with browser testing for responsive behavior and real interaction checks.
anti_patterns: Using broad repository patterns that include non-UI code and reduce review focus.
Treating the audit as a replacement for accessibility testing with assistive technology.
Ignoring the remote guideline dependency when reviewing sensitive or offline projects.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews web interface files against Vercel Web Interface Guidelines and reports design or accessibility issues.

faq.question: Does it need internet access?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill fetches the latest guideline document from a raw GitHub URL before each review.

faq.question: Can it review an entire project?
faq.answer: It can review broad patterns, but smaller file sets usually produce clearer and more useful findings.

faq.question: Does it change my code?
faq.answer: No. The skill is designed to report findings, not apply edits automatically.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is the guideline source fixed?
faq.answer: No. It fetches fresh remote content, so the review criteria can change when the upstream document changes.
